evals.prompt: What companies raised funding this week?
evals.expected_output: A routed Brave Search news-search for recent funding-round announcements, returning news articles about recently funded companies.
assertions: The skill issues a single routed (MPP) step to https://brave.mpp.paywithlocus.com/brave/news-search
The request body sets q to a query like '<sector> startup funding round announced'
The output lists recent news articles announcing funding rounds (title, source, date, URL), not structured deal records

evals.prompt: Show me seed rounds from the last month.
evals.expected_output: A routed Brave news-search whose query wording biases toward seed rounds, since news-search has no structured round-type filter.
assertions: The body's q includes a seed-round phrasing (e.g. 'seed' in the sector wording)
No date_start/date_end or financing_types fields are sent — recency comes from the news index
The step rail is routed and method is POST

evals.prompt: What fintech companies recently raised a Series A?
evals.expected_output: A routed Brave news-search with a query combining fintech and Series A wording.
assertions: The body's q mentions fintech and Series A
Company names and deal details are extracted from the returned articles rather than structured fields

evals.prompt: List the biggest funding rounds over $10M from the past week.
evals.expected_output: A routed Brave news-search; deal-size filtering happens client-side by reading the articles, since news-search has no size_min filter.
assertions: No size_min field is sent; size filtering is done from article text
The per-step maxAmount of 0.40 comfortably covers the $0.03675 live price and is not exceeded
The skill uses only the routed rail with no direct or x402 step

evals.prompt: Validate the recent funding rounds endpoint without paying.
evals.expected_output: A free 402 probe against the Brave Search news-search endpoint via selat-pay --probe-only.
assertions: The validation command uses selat-pay POST https://brave.mpp.paywithlocus.com/brave/news-search with --probe-only
No payment is broadcast during the probe
The output reflects the 402 payment requirements rather than a 200 data response

evals.prompt: How does this skill call Brave Search under the hood?
evals.expected_output: The skill is declarative: the selat CLI compiles the manifest step into a payment; it never shells out to orth or api.orth.sh.
assertions: The manifest contains no orth, orth run, or api.orth.sh references
Execution is via selat skill run / selat-pay over the SELAT Router
The single step is a plain method+url+body JSON object
